purpose: Quantifies the fundamental driver of services inflation persistence through unit labor costs and identifies conditions required for resolution
components: Services wage growth at 4.0-4.5% as the primary input
Unit labor cost growth = wage growth minus productivity = 3.0-4.0%
Calvo pricing mechanism transmitting ULC to services CPI at 3.5-4.0%
Three resolution channels: wage deceleration (labor market softening), productivity acceleration (AI timeline), margin compression (limited scope)
current_assessment: Services ULC growth at 3.0-4.0% fully explains current persistence without appeal to structural breaks. None of the three resolution channels are operating at sufficient scale within the 2026-2028 horizon. The model correctly predicts the level but does not explain why transmission is slower this cycle.
limitations: Calvo pricing assumption may not capture full heterogeneity of services sector pricing
Does not account for transmission impairment or credit channel delay
Productivity measurement in services is notoriously difficult
Aggregate model may miss sector-specific dynamics (PE consolidation, insurance)
